name: delphi
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "use delphi", "ask delphi", or wants multiple parallel oracle investigations of the same question to discover divergent insights. Delphi launches multiple oracle agents simultaneously with identical prompts, allowing them to independently explore and potentially discover different paths, clues, and solutions. Results are saved to .oracle/[topic]/ and synthesized into a final document.
capabilities: Launches 3-5 parallel AI agents with identical prompts
Creates structured investigation reports in .oracle directories
Synthesizes divergent findings into comprehensive analysis
Exports complete reasoning chains from each oracle agent
Identifies convergent and divergent findings across investigations
author: Cygnusfear
securitySummary: The delphi skill is documentation-only with no executable code. Static findings are false positives: SHA-256 content hashes in the audit file were flagged as weak crypto, markdown code formatting was flagged as shell execution, and git branch names were flagged as C2 keywords. All findings dismissed after human review.
valueStatement: Complex problems need multiple perspectives. Delphi coordinates parallel AI agents to explore the same question independently, revealing divergent insights you might miss with a single investigation.
limitations: Requires manual coordination of parallel Task calls
Generates multiple large investigation files per consultation
Synthesis quality depends on individual oracle thoroughness
No automatic retry mechanism for failed oracle agents

useCases.title: Evaluate architecture decisions
useCases.description: Get multiple expert perspectives on complex technical choices before implementation

useCases.title: Investigate security incidents
useCases.description: Parallel investigation of breach vectors reveals overlooked attack paths

useCases.title: Analyze market opportunities
useCases.description: Comprehensive market analysis from different strategic viewpoints

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use delphi to investigate whether our e-commerce platform should migrate from monolith to microservices. Consider performance, scalability, team structure, and migration complexity.

promptTemplates.title: Bug Investigation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use delphi to investigate why our payment processing randomly fails for 0.1 percent of transactions. Check logs, recent changes, dependencies, and infrastructure.

promptTemplates.title: Research Synthesis
promptTemplates.prompt: Use delphi to research the current state of WebAssembly for server-side applications. Include performance benchmarks, adoption trends, and production use cases.

promptTemplates.title: Root Cause Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Use delphi to investigate the root cause of last week database performance degradation. Analyze query patterns, configuration changes, and infrastructure metrics.

bestPractices: Provide clear success criteria in your oracle prompts
Create descriptive topic names for the .oracle directory
Let oracles explore independently without predetermined paths
antiPatterns: Do not assign different focus areas to each oracle
Avoid launching too many oracles that become hard to synthesize
Do not skip the synthesis step - individual reports are not the final answer

faq.question: How many oracles should I use?
faq.answer: 3-5 oracles work best. More than 5 creates synthesis challenges without proportional benefit.

faq.question: Can oracles communicate with each other?
faq.answer: No, oracles work independently. This ensures divergent exploration without groupthink.

faq.question: What if an oracle fails or times out?
faq.answer: Check the .oracle directory for partial results. Consider relaunching failed oracles with adjusted prompts.

faq.question: How long does a Delphi consultation take?
faq.answer: Each oracle runs 5-15 minutes. Total time depends on complexity and oracle count.

faq.question: Can I use different models for different oracles?
faq.answer: Yes, but identical models ensure consistent capability across investigations.

faq.question: What makes a good oracle prompt?
faq.answer: Include the core question, relevant context, success criteria, and export instructions.
